label: Now Developing
items: Holy Week closed with record travel volumes, road-crash injuries, and scam warnings — one movement surge exposed pressure across transport, health, and border systems.
March inflation accelerated to 4.1% as fuel shock moved into household math, with transport inflation at 9.9% and diesel nearing ₱148.60/L in Metro Manila trackers.
The first meaningful rollback signal arrived for April 14-20, with DOE projecting cuts up to ₱20.89/L for diesel — relief, but not yet a full reset.
